description: Tracks which endpoint (primary vs failover) each edge node is probing — shows failover events and their latency impact

panels.description: Current active probe target for each edge node (primary or backup). Changes when mitigator issues failover_endpoint command.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT device_id, target_id
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts = (
  SELECT MAX(ts) FROM telemetry_measurements t2
  WHERE t2.device_id = telemetry_measurements.device_id
    AND t2.ts >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz
)
AND ts >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz
ORDER BY device_id;

panels.title: Current Probe Target per Node

panels.description: Number of HTTP probe measurements per minute to the PRIMARY endpoint per node. When a node failsover this line drops to zero.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  device_id,
  count(*) AS probe_count
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'primary'
  AND http_ok IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Probes → PRIMARY Endpoint (per node/min)

panels.description: Number of HTTP probe measurements per minute to the FAILOVER endpoint per node. Non-zero only after a failover_endpoint mitigation command is applied.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  device_id,
  count(*) AS probe_count
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'backup'
  AND http_ok IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Probes → FAILOVER Endpoint (per node/min)

panels.description: Combined stacked view: total probes to primary (blue) vs failover (green) across all nodes. The crossover point is when the mitigator fired.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  'Primary Probes' AS metric,
  count(*) AS value
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'primary'
  AND http_ok IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
UNION ALL
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  'Failover Probes' AS metric,
  count(*) AS value
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'backup'
  AND http_ok IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: All Nodes — Primary vs Failover Probe Volume (Stacked)

panels.description: HTTP latency comparison: primary endpoint (affected by fault injection) vs failover endpoint (clean path). The latency drop after failover shows the impact reduction.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  device_id || ' [primary]' AS metric,
  round(avg(http_latency_ms)::numeric, 1) AS value
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'primary'
  AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL
  AND ($device_id = 'all' OR device_id = ANY(ARRAY[$device_id]::text[]))
GROUP BY 1, 2
UNION ALL
SELECT
  date_trunc('minute', ts) AS time,
  device_id || ' [backup]' AS metric,
  round(avg(http_latency_ms)::numeric, 1) AS value
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'backup'
  AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL
  AND ($device_id = 'all' OR device_id = ANY(ARRAY[$device_id]::text[]))
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: HTTP Latency — Primary vs Failover per Node

panels.description: Mean HTTP latency while probing primary vs failover endpoint across the selected time window. Lower backup latency confirms the failover mitigation is effective.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT 'Primary' AS endpoint, round(avg(http_latency_ms)::numeric,1) AS avg_latency_ms
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'primary' AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Failover' AS endpoint, round(avg(http_latency_ms)::numeric,1) AS avg_latency_ms
FROM telemetry_measurements
WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND target_id = 'backup' AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL;

panels.title: Avg HTTP Latency — Primary vs Failover

panels.description: Impact reduction = percentage improvement in HTTP latency after switching to the failover endpoint. Positive value means failover is faster than primary (during fault).

targets.rawSql:
WITH primary_avg AS (
  SELECT avg(http_latency_ms) AS lat
  FROM telemetry_measurements
  WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
    AND target_id = 'primary' AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL
),
backup_avg AS (
  SELECT avg(http_latency_ms) AS lat
  FROM telemetry_measurements
  WHERE ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
    AND target_id = 'backup' AND http_latency_ms IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  round(((p.lat - b.lat) / NULLIF(p.lat, 0) * 100)::numeric, 1) AS impact_reduction_pct
FROM primary_avg p, backup_avg b;

panels.title: Failover Impact Reduction %

panels.description: All mitigation actions of type failover_endpoint — shows exactly when each node was switched to the backup endpoint.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  issued_ts,
  device_id,
  action,
  status,
  status_ts,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (status_ts - issued_ts)) * 1000 AS ack_latency_ms,
  params::text AS params
FROM mitigation_actions
WHERE issued_ts BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AND $__timeTo()::timestamptz
  AND action IN ('failover_endpoint', 'set_interval')
ORDER BY issued_ts DESC
LIMIT 100;

panels.title: Failover & Interval Commands — Full Event Log

list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT device_id FROM telemetry_measurements ORDER BY device_id
list.label: Node
list.name: device_id
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT device_id FROM telemetry_measurements ORDER BY device_id

title: Probe Endpoint Tracking — Primary vs Failover
